rationale_for_baseline_tier: A well, afebrile patient with a blanching, classic post-streptococcal guttate eruption and no anaphylaxis features is low acuity — reassurance, emollient/topical steroid and routine GP/dermatology follow-up. The only escalations are for anaphylaxis or a non-blanching febrile rash, both currently absent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Lip/tongue/face swelling, wheeze, throat tightness, or trouble breathing
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your lips, tongue or face swell, you wheeze, or you have trouble breathing, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rash becomes non-blanching (stays under a glass) with fever or feeling very unwell
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the spots stop fading when you press a glass on them and you develop a fever or feel very unwell, call 000 or go to ED immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Explicitly clarify this is not a drug allergy to avoid an inappropriate lifelong label.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is guttate psoriasis triggered by your recent throat infection — not a penicillin allergy and not contagious. Moisturise well, you can use a mild steroid cream, and see your GP within a week or two. Call 000 if your lips/face swell or you wheeze, or if the spots stop fading under a glass and you get a fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1-2 weeks; 000 for anaphylaxis or non-blanching febrile rash
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No emergency care needed unless a safety-net feature appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth impression of post-streptococcal guttate psoriasis; anaphylaxis and meningococcaemia excluded (blanching rash, afebrile, no angioedema); NOT a penicillin allergy; emollient/topical steroid advised; routine dermatology follow-up.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring with no anaphylaxis/meningococcal screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A genuinely evolving allergic or meningococcal process is missed because red-flag features were never screened.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED escalation for a well patient with a blanching post-strep rash is over-triage; a lifelong penicillin-allergy label is a separate harm.
